verses.greek: Παῦλος ἀπόστολος, οὐκ ἀπ’ ἀνθρώπων οὐδὲ δι’ ἀνθρώπου ἀλλὰ διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ καὶ θεοῦ πατρὸς τοῦ ἐγείραντος αὐτὸν ἐκ νεκρῶν,
verses.mistral: Paul, an apostle, not from humans nor through human agency, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead.
verses.kjv: Paul, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ σὺν ἐμοὶ πάντες ἀδελφοί, ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς Γαλατίας·
verses.mistral: And all who are with me, brethren, to the churches of Galatia:
verses.kjv: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:

verses.greek: χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ⸂καὶ κυρίου ἡμῶν⸃ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,

verses.greek: τοῦ δόντος ἑαυτὸν ⸀ὑπὲρ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν ὅπως ἐξέληται ἡμᾶς ἐκ τοῦ ⸂αἰῶνος τοῦ ἐνεστῶτος⸃ πονηροῦ κατὰ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ πατρὸς ἡμῶν,
verses.mistral: Given, who gave Himself for our sins so that He might deliver us from the present evil age according to the will of God and our Father.
verses.kjv: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:

verses.greek: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: To the ages of the ages, Amen.
verses.kjv: To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.greek: Θαυμάζω ὅτι οὕτως ταχέως μετατίθεσθε ἀπὸ τοῦ καλέσαντος ὑμᾶς ἐν χάριτι Χριστοῦ εἰς ἕτερον εὐαγγέλιον,
verses.mistral: Swiftly transitioning from the grace of Christ to another gospel
verses.kjv: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:

verses.greek: ὃ οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλο· εἰ μή τινές εἰσιν οἱ ταράσσοντες ὑμᾶς καὶ θέλοντες μεταστρέψαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Another gospel is not being preached, if indeed some are disturbing you and trying to pervert the gospel of Christ.
verses.kjv: Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐὰν ἡμεῖς ἢ ἄγγελος ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ⸀εὐαγγελίζηται ⸀ὑμῖν παρ’ ὃ εὐηγγελισάμεθα ὑμῖν, ἀνάθεμα ἔστω.
verses.mistral: If even an angel from heaven preaches to you a gospel contrary to what we have preached to you, let him be accursed.
verses.kjv: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.

verses.greek: ὡς προειρήκαμεν, καὶ ἄρτι πάλιν λέγω, εἴ τις ὑμᾶς εὐαγγελίζεται παρ’ ὃ παρελάβετε, ἀνάθεμα ἔστω.
verses.mistral: As we have stated before, and now I say again: If anyone preaches to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let him be accursed.
verses.kjv: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.

verses.greek: Ἄρτι γὰρ ἀνθρώπους πείθω ἢ τὸν θεόν; ἢ ζητῶ ἀνθρώποις ἀρέσκειν; ⸀εἰ ἔτι ἀνθρώποις ἤρεσκον, Χριστοῦ δοῦλος οὐκ ἂν ἤμην.
verses.mistral: People please or God? Do I still please people? If so, I would not be a servant of Christ.
verses.kjv: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

verses.greek: Γνωρίζω ⸀γὰρ ὑμῖν, ἀδελφοί, τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τὸ εὐαγγελισθὲν ὑπ’ ἐμοῦ ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν κατὰ ἄνθρωπον·
verses.mistral: Announce (I) to you, brethren, the Gospel which I preached, that it is not according to man.
verses.kjv: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.

verses.greek: οὐδὲ γὰρ ἐγὼ παρὰ ἀνθρώπου παρέλαβον αὐτό, ⸀οὔτε ἐδιδάχθην, ἀλλὰ δι’ ἀποκαλύψεως Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Received not I from man, nor was I taught, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: Ἠκούσατε γὰρ τὴν ἐμὴν ἀναστροφήν ποτε ἐν τῷ Ἰουδαϊσμῷ, ὅτι καθ’ ὑπερβολὴν ἐδίωκον τὴν ἐκκλησίαν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἐπόρθουν αὐτήν,
verses.mistral: You heard of my former life in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God exceedingly and tried to destroy it.
verses.kjv: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews' religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:

verses.greek: καὶ προέκοπτον ἐν τῷ Ἰουδαϊσμῷ ὑπὲρ πολλοὺς συνηλικιώτας ἐν τῷ γένει μου, περισσοτέρως ζηλωτὴς ὑπάρχων τῶν πατρικῶν μου παραδόσεων.
verses.mistral: I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my own age among their nation, exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
verses.kjv: And profited in the Jews' religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ⸀εὐδόκησεν ὁ ἀφορίσας με ἐκ κοιλίας μητρός μου καὶ καλέσας διὰ τῆς χάριτος αὐτοῦ
verses.mistral: When God, who had set me apart from my mother's womb and called me by His grace...
verses.kjv: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother's womb, and called me by his grace,

verses.greek: ἀποκαλύψαι τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἐν ἐμοὶ ἵνα εὐαγγελίζωμαι αὐτὸν ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν, εὐθέως οὐ προσανεθέμην σαρκὶ καὶ αἵματι,
verses.mistral: Reveal my Son in me, that I might proclaim Him among the Gentiles. Immediately I did not confer with flesh and blood
verses.kjv: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:

verses.greek: οὐδὲ ἀνῆλθον εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα πρὸς τοὺς πρὸ ἐμοῦ ἀποστόλους, ἀλλὰ ἀπῆλθον εἰς Ἀραβίαν, καὶ πάλιν ὑπέστρεψα εἰς Δαμασκόν.
verses.mistral: I did not go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went to Arabia, and again I returned to Damascus.
verses.kjv: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.

verses.greek: Ἔπειτα μετὰ ⸂ἔτη τρία⸃ ἀνῆλθον εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα ἱστορῆσαι ⸀Κηφᾶν, καὶ ἐπέμεινα πρὸς αὐτὸν ἡμέρας δεκαπέντε·
verses.mistral: After three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas, and I stayed with him fifteen days.
verses.kjv: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.

verses.greek: ἕτερον δὲ τῶν ἀποστόλων οὐκ εἶδον, εἰ μὴ Ἰάκωβον τὸν ἀδελφὸν τοῦ κυρίου.
verses.mistral: Another apostle I did not see, except James, the brother of the Lord.
verses.kjv: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord's brother.

verses.greek: ἃ δὲ γράφω ὑμῖν, ἰδοὺ ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ ὅτι οὐ ψεύδομαι.
verses.mistral: I am telling you the truth before God.
verses.kjv: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.

verses.greek: ἔπειτα ἦλθον εἰς τὰ κλίματα τῆς Συρίας καὶ τῆς Κιλικίας.
verses.mistral: Then I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
verses.kjv: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;

verses.greek: ἤμην δὲ ἀγνοούμενος τῷ προσώπῳ ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς Ἰουδαίας ταῖς ἐν Χριστῷ,
verses.mistral: I was unknown by face to the churches of Judea in Christ.
verses.kjv: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:

verses.greek: μόνον δὲ ἀκούοντες ἦσαν ὅτι Ὁ διώκων ἡμᾶς ποτε νῦν εὐαγγελίζεται τὴν πίστιν ἥν ποτε ἐπόρθει,
verses.mistral: Now persecutor, now proclaims the faith which once persecuted.
verses.kjv: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐδόξαζον ἐν ἐμοὶ τὸν θεόν.
verses.mistral: And glorified in me God.
verses.kjv: And they glorified God in me.

verses.greek: Ἔπειτα διὰ δεκατεσσάρων ἐτῶν πάλιν ἀνέβην εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα μετὰ Βαρναβᾶ συμπαραλαβὼν καὶ Τίτον·
verses.mistral: After fourteen years, I again went up to Jerusalem with Barnabas, also taking Titus along.
verses.kjv: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.

verses.greek: ἀνέβην δὲ κατὰ ἀποκάλυψιν· καὶ ἀνεθέμην αὐτοῖς τὸ εὐαγγέλιον ὃ κηρύσσω ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν, κατ’ ἰδίαν δὲ τοῖς δοκοῦσιν, μή πως εἰς κενὸν τρέχω ἢ ἔδραμον.
verses.mistral: I was personally revealed to them; I shared the gospel that I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to those who were of repute, lest I should be running in vain or have run in vain.
verses.kjv: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ οὐδὲ Τίτος ὁ σὺν ἐμοί, Ἕλλην ὤν, ἠναγκάσθη περιτμηθῆναι·
verses.mistral: Titus, who was with me, was not compelled to be circumcised.
verses.kjv: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:

verses.greek: διὰ δὲ τοὺς παρεισάκτους ψευδαδέλφους, οἵτινες παρεισῆλθον κατασκοπῆσαι τὴν ἐλευθερίαν ἡμῶν ἣν ἔχομεν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ, ἵνα ἡμᾶς ⸀καταδουλώσουσιν—
verses.mistral: Despite the false brothers who have infiltrated to examine our freedom that we have in Christ Jesus, so as to enslave us
verses.kjv: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:

verses.greek: οἷς οὐδὲ πρὸς ὥραν εἴξαμεν τῇ ὑποταγῇ, ἵνα ἡ ἀλήθεια τοῦ εὐαγγελίου διαμείνῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: We have not submitted to their regime, so that the truth of the Gospel remains with you.
verses.kjv: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.

verses.greek: ἀπὸ δὲ τῶν δοκούντων εἶναί τι— ὁποῖοί ποτε ἦσαν οὐδέν μοι διαφέρει· πρόσωπον ⸀θεὸς ἀνθρώπου οὐ λαμβάνει— ἐμοὶ γὰρ οἱ δοκοῦντες οὐδὲν προσανέθεντο,
verses.mistral: From those who seem to be something— as though they were something— it makes no difference to me. God shows no favoritism— for those who seemed worthy of nothing, not even I received anything from them.
verses.kjv: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man's person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ τοὐναντίον ἰδόντες ὅτι πεπίστευμαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς ἀκροβυστίας καθὼς Πέτρος τῆς περιτομῆς,
verses.mistral: But contrastingly, seeing that I believe in the gospel of the uncircumcision as Peter did in the circumcision,
verses.kjv: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ ἐνεργήσας Πέτρῳ εἰς ἀποστολὴν τῆς περιτομῆς ἐνήργησεν καὶ ἐμοὶ εἰς τὰ ἔθνη,
verses.mistral: Worked in Peter for the apostleship of circumcision, worked also in me among the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)

verses.greek: καὶ γνόντες τὴν χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι, Ἰάκωβος καὶ Κηφᾶς καὶ Ἰωάννης, οἱ δοκοῦντες στῦλοι εἶναι, δεξιὰς ἔδωκαν ἐμοὶ καὶ Βαρναβᾷ κοινωνίας, ἵνα ⸀ἡμεῖς εἰς τὰ ἔθνη, αὐτοὶ δὲ εἰς τὴν περιτομήν·
verses.mistral: Recognizing the grace given to me, Jacob and Cephas and John, who seemed to be pillars, granted me fellowship on an equal level with themselves, so that I might go among the Gentiles, but they for circumcision.
verses.kjv: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.

verses.greek: μόνον τῶν πτωχῶν ἵνα μνημονεύωμεν, ὃ καὶ ἐσπούδασα αὐτὸ τοῦτο ποιῆσαι.
verses.mistral: Remember the poor, for this I also was eager to do.
verses.kjv: Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.

verses.greek: Ὅτε δὲ ἦλθεν ⸀Κηφᾶς εἰς Ἀντιόχειαν, κατὰ πρόσωπον αὐτῷ ἀντέστην, ὅτι κατεγνωσμένος ἦν·
verses.mistral: When Peter came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, for he was clearly in the wrong.
verses.kjv: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.

verses.greek: πρὸ τοῦ γὰρ ἐλθεῖν τινας ἀπὸ Ἰακώβου μετὰ τῶν ἐθνῶν συνήσθιεν· ὅτε δὲ ⸀ἦλθον, ὑπέστελλεν καὶ ἀφώριζεν ἑαυτόν, φοβούμενος τοὺς ἐκ περιτομῆς.
verses.mistral: Did eat with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. When he arrived, he began to separate and hold himself aloof, fearing those of circumcision.
verses.kjv: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.

verses.greek: καὶ συνυπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ ⸀καὶ οἱ λοιποὶ Ἰουδαῖοι, ὥστε καὶ Βαρναβᾶς συναπήχθη αὐτῶν τῇ ὑποκρίσει.
verses.mistral: And even Barnabas was carried away with their deceit.
verses.kjv: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ὅτε εἶδον ὅτι οὐκ ὀρθοποδοῦσιν πρὸς τὴν ἀλήθειαν τοῦ εὐαγγελίου, εἶπον τῷ ⸀Κηφᾷ ἔμπροσθεν πάντων· Εἰ σὺ Ἰουδαῖος ὑπάρχων ἐθνικῶς ⸂καὶ ⸀οὐκ Ἰουδαϊκῶς ζῇς⸃, ⸀πῶς τὰ ἔθνη ἀναγκάζεις Ἰουδαΐζειν;
verses.mistral: But when I saw that they were not walking according to the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone: "If you, being a Jew, live not as a Jew but as one among the Gentiles, why do you compel the Gentiles to Judaize?"
verses.kjv: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?

verses.greek: Ἡμεῖς φύσει Ἰουδαῖοι καὶ οὐκ ἐξ ἐθνῶν ἁμαρτωλοί,
verses.mistral: We Jews by nature are not sinners
verses.kjv: We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,

verses.greek: εἰδότες ⸀δὲ ὅτι οὐ δικαιοῦται ἄνθρωπος ἐξ ἔργων νόμου ἐὰν μὴ διὰ πίστεως ⸂Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ⸃, καὶ ἡμεῖς εἰς Χριστὸν Ἰησοῦν ἐπιστεύσαμεν, ἵνα δικαιωθῶμεν ἐκ πίστεως Χριστοῦ καὶ οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων νόμου, ⸂ὅτι ἐξ ἔργων νόμου οὐ δικαιωθήσεται⸃ πᾶσα σάρξ.
verses.mistral: Humans are not justified by the works of the law, but by faith in Jesus Christ. We have believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ and not by the works of the law, for by the works of the law no flesh will be justified.
verses.kjv: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ζητοῦντες δικαιωθῆναι ἐν Χριστῷ εὑρέθημεν καὶ αὐτοὶ ἁμαρτωλοί, ἆρα Χριστὸς ἁμαρτίας διάκονος; μὴ γένοιτο·
verses.mistral: If seeking to be justified in Christ we were found also to be sinners, is Christ a minister of sin? Certainly not.
verses.kjv: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ ἃ κατέλυσα ταῦτα πάλιν οἰκοδομῶ, παραβάτην ἐμαυτὸν ⸀συνιστάνω.
verses.mistral: I am reinforcing what was destroyed, I am affirming myself as a transgressor.
verses.kjv: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.

verses.greek: ἐγὼ γὰρ διὰ νόμου νόμῳ ἀπέθανον ἵνα θεῷ ζήσω· Χριστῷ συνεσταύρωμαι·
verses.mistral: I have died through the law, that I might live for God; I am crucified with Christ.
verses.kjv: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.

verses.greek: ζῶ δὲ οὐκέτι ἐγώ, ζῇ δὲ ἐν ἐμοὶ Χριστός· ὃ δὲ νῦν ζῶ ἐν σαρκί, ἐν πίστει ζῶ τῇ τοῦ ⸂υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ⸃ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντός με καὶ παραδόντος ἑαυτὸν ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ.
verses.mistral: I no longer live, but Christ lives in me; and the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
verses.kjv: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.

verses.greek: οὐκ ἀθετῶ τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ· εἰ γὰρ διὰ νόμου δικαιοσύνη, ἄρα Χριστὸς δωρεὰν ἀπέθανεν.
verses.mistral: Do not nullify the grace of God; for if righteousness comes through the law, then Christ died in vain.
verses.kjv: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.

verses.greek: Ὦ ἀνόητοι Γαλάται, τίς ὑμᾶς ⸀ἐβάσκανεν, οἷς κατ’ ὀφθαλμοὺς Ἰησοῦς Χριστὸς ⸀προεγράφη ἐσταυρωμένος;
verses.mistral: Oh foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you, to whom Jesus Christ was publicly portrayed as crucified?
verses.kjv: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?

verses.greek: τοῦτο μόνον θέλω μαθεῖν ἀφ’ ὑμῶν, ἐξ ἔργων νόμου τὸ πνεῦμα ἐλάβετε ἢ ἐξ ἀκοῆς πίστεως;
verses.mistral: From you I desire to learn this alone: Did you receive the Spirit by works of the law, or by hearing of faith?
verses.kjv: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

verses.greek: οὕτως ἀνόητοί ἐστε; ἐναρξάμενοι πνεύματι νῦν σαρκὶ ἐπιτελεῖσθε;
verses.mistral: Thus, you are foolish. Have you begun in the Spirit, and now are you being made perfect by flesh?
verses.kjv: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?

verses.greek: τοσαῦτα ἐπάθετε εἰκῇ; εἴ γε καὶ εἰκῇ.
verses.mistral: You have suffered as by a scheme. Even so, by a scheme.
verses.kjv: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.

verses.greek: ὁ οὖν ἐπιχορηγῶν ὑμῖν τὸ πνεῦμα καὶ ἐνεργῶν δυνάμεις ἐν ὑμῖν ἐξ ἔργων νόμου ἢ ἐξ ἀκοῆς πίστεως;
verses.mistral: Who is supplying the spirit and working miracles among you by the works of the law or by hearing of faith?
verses.kjv: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

verses.greek: καθὼς Ἀβραὰμ ἐπίστευσεν τῷ θεῷ, καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην.
verses.mistral: Justified is Abraham by faith.
verses.kjv: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.

verses.greek: Γινώσκετε ἄρα ὅτι οἱ ἐκ πίστεως, οὗτοι ⸂υἱοί εἰσιν⸃ Ἀβραάμ.
verses.mistral: Children of faith are Abraham.
verses.kjv: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.

verses.greek: προϊδοῦσα δὲ ἡ γραφὴ ὅτι ἐκ πίστεως δικαιοῖ τὰ ἔθνη ὁ θεὸς προευηγγελίσατο τῷ Ἀβραὰμ ὅτι Ἐνευλογηθήσονται ἐν σοὶ πάντα τὰ ἔθνη.
verses.mistral: Predetermined indeed is the scripture, that from faith God justifies the nations. He announced to Abraham that in you all the nations will be blessed.
verses.kjv: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.

verses.greek: ὥστε οἱ ἐκ πίστεως εὐλογοῦνται σὺν τῷ πιστῷ Ἀβραάμ.
verses.mistral: The blessed are those from faith along with the faithful Abraham.
verses.kjv: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.

verses.greek: Ὅσοι γὰρ ἐξ ἔργων νόμου εἰσὶν ὑπὸ κατάραν εἰσίν, γέγραπται γὰρ ⸀ὅτι Ἐπικατάρατος πᾶς ὃς οὐκ ⸀ἐμμένει πᾶσιν τοῖς γεγραμμένοις ἐν τῷ βιβλίῳ τοῦ νόμου τοῦ ποιῆσαι αὐτά.
verses.mistral: Condemned are those who are from works of the law, as it is written: "Cursed is everyone who does not continue to do everything written in the book of the law to perform it."
verses.kjv: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.

verses.greek: ὅτι δὲ ἐν νόμῳ οὐδεὶς δικαιοῦται παρὰ τῷ θεῷ δῆλον, ὅτι Ὁ δίκαιος ἐκ πίστεως ζήσεται,
verses.mistral: The righteous shall live by faith.
verses.kjv: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ νόμος οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ πίστεως, ἀλλ’· Ὁ ποιήσας ⸀αὐτὰ ζήσεται ἐν αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: The law is not from faith, but the one doing them shall live by them.
verses.kjv: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.

verses.greek: Χριστὸς ἡμᾶς ἐξηγόρασεν ἐκ τῆς κατάρας τοῦ νόμου γενόμενος ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν κατάρα, ⸂ὅτι γέγραπται⸃· Ἐπικατάρατος πᾶς ὁ κρεμάμενος ἐπὶ ξύλου,
verses.mistral: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, becoming a curse for us, as it is written: "Cursed is everyone who is hung on a tree."
verses.kjv: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:

verses.greek: ἵνα εἰς τὰ ἔθνη ἡ εὐλογία τοῦ Ἀβραὰμ γένηται ἐν ⸂Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ⸃, ἵνα τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν τοῦ πνεύματος λάβωμεν διὰ τῆς πίστεως.
verses.mistral: In Christ Jesus, the blessing of Abraham might come upon the Gentiles, that we may receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
verses.kjv: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

verses.greek: Ἀδελφοί, κατὰ ἄνθρωπον λέγω· ὅμως ἀνθρώπου κεκυρωμένην διαθήκην οὐδεὶς ἀθετεῖ ἢ ἐπιδιατάσσεται.
verses.mistral: Brothers, I speak as a man: though no one may annul or add to a man's covenant.
verses.kjv: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man's covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ Ἀβραὰμ ἐρρέθησαν αἱ ἐπαγγελίαι καὶ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ· οὐ λέγει· Καὶ τοῖς σπέρμασιν, ὡς ἐπὶ πολλῶν, ἀλλ’ ὡς ἐφ’ ἑνός· Καὶ τῷ σπέρματί σου, ὅς ἐστιν Χριστός.
verses.mistral: The promises were made to Abraham and to his seed. Not saying, "And to the seeds," as of many, but as of one. And to your Seed, who is Christ.
verses.kjv: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

verses.greek: τοῦτο δὲ λέγω· διαθήκην προκεκυρωμένην ὑπὸ τοῦ ⸀θεοῦ ὁ μετὰ ⸂τετρακόσια καὶ τριάκοντα ἔτη⸃ γεγονὼς νόμος οὐκ ἀκυροῖ, εἰς τὸ καταργῆσαι τὴν ἐπαγγελίαν.
verses.mistral: This says: "A covenant confirmed before by God remains valid, and the law does not nullify the promise."
verses.kjv: And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ ἐκ νόμου ἡ κληρονομία, οὐκέτι ἐξ ἐπαγγελίας· τῷ δὲ Ἀβραὰμ δι’ ἐπαγγελίας κεχάρισται ὁ θεός.
verses.mistral: The promise is by virtue of faith in Abraham, God has granted him.
verses.kjv: For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.

verses.greek: Τί οὖν ὁ νόμος; τῶν παραβάσεων χάριν προσετέθη, ἄχρις ⸀οὗ ἔλθῃ τὸ σπέρμα ᾧ ἐπήγγελται, διαταγεὶς δι’ ἀγγέλων ἐν χειρὶ μεσίτου·
verses.mistral: What is the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the seed to whom the promise was made would come; it was administered through angels by a mediator.
verses.kjv: Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ μεσίτης ἑνὸς οὐκ ἔστιν, ὁ δὲ θεὸς εἷς ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: The mediator of one is not, but God is one.
verses.kjv: Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.

verses.greek: Ὁ οὖν νόμος κατὰ τῶν ἐπαγγελιῶν τοῦ θεοῦ; μὴ γένοιτο· εἰ γὰρ ἐδόθη νόμος ὁ δυνάμενος ζῳοποιῆσαι, ὄντως ⸂ἐκ νόμου ἂν⸃ ἦν ἡ δικαιοσύνη.
verses.mistral: The law according to the covenant of God is not; God forbid! If indeed a law were given that could make alive, truly it would have been from law.
verses.kjv: Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ συνέκλεισεν ἡ γραφὴ τὰ πάντα ὑπὸ ἁμαρτίαν ἵνα ἡ ἐπαγγελία ἐκ πίστεως Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ δοθῇ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν.
verses.mistral: But the scripture shut up all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those believing.
verses.kjv: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.

verses.greek: Πρὸ τοῦ δὲ ἐλθεῖν τὴν πίστιν ὑπὸ νόμον ἐφρουρούμεθα ⸀συγκλειόμενοι εἰς τὴν μέλλουσαν πίστιν ἀποκαλυφθῆναι.
verses.mistral: Previously, we were held captive under the law, imprisoned until the coming faith would be revealed.
verses.kjv: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

verses.greek: ὥστε ὁ νόμος παιδαγωγὸς ἡμῶν γέγονεν εἰς Χριστόν, ἵνα ἐκ πίστεως δικαιωθῶμεν·
verses.mistral: The law has become our tutor to lead us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
verses.kjv: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

verses.greek: ἐλθούσης δὲ τῆς πίστεως οὐκέτι ὑπὸ παιδαγωγόν ἐσμεν.
verses.mistral: We are no longer under a tutor (law).
verses.kjv: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ υἱοὶ θεοῦ ἐστε διὰ τῆς πίστεως ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: All are sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: ὅσοι γὰρ εἰς Χριστὸν ἐβαπτίσθητε, Χριστὸν ἐνεδύσασθε·
verses.mistral: You have been clothed with Christ.
verses.kjv: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔνι Ἰουδαῖος οὐδὲ Ἕλλην, οὐκ ἔνι δοῦλος οὐδὲ ἐλεύθερος, οὐκ ἔνι ἄρσεν καὶ θῆλυ· ⸀πάντες γὰρ ὑμεῖς εἷς ἐστε ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: All of you are one in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ὑμεῖς Χριστοῦ, ἄρα τοῦ Ἀβραὰμ σπέρμα ἐστέ, ⸀κατ’ ἐπαγγελίαν κληρονόμοι.
verses.mistral: If you are Christ's, indeed you are the seed of Abraham, heirs according to promise.
verses.kjv: And if ye be Christ's, then are ye Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise.

verses.greek: Λέγω δέ, ἐφ’ ὅσον χρόνον ὁ κληρονόμος νήπιός ἐστιν, οὐδὲν διαφέρει δούλου κύριος πάντων ὤν,
verses.mistral: Child, as long as the heir is a minor, he is no different from a slave—all the same, he is under guardians and managers.
verses.kjv: Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ὑπὸ ἐπιτρόπους ἐστὶ καὶ οἰκονόμους ἄχρι τῆς προθεσμίας τοῦ πατρός.
verses.mistral: Under guardians and managers he remains until the will of the father takes effect.
verses.kjv: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ἡμεῖς, ὅτε ἦμεν νήπιοι, ὑπὸ τὰ στοιχεῖα τοῦ κόσμου ⸀ἤμεθα δεδουλωμένοι·
verses.mistral: Thus we also, when we were children, were under the elements of the world.
verses.kjv: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ἦλθεν τὸ πλήρωμα τοῦ χρόνου, ἐξαπέστειλεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ, γενόμενον ἐκ γυναικός, γενόμενον ὑπὸ νόμον,
verses.mistral: When the fullness of time came, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under law.
verses.kjv: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,

verses.greek: ἵνα τοὺς ὑπὸ νόμον ἐξαγοράσῃ, ἵνα τὴν υἱοθεσίαν ἀπολάβωμεν.
verses.mistral: So that we might be redeemed from the law, so that we might receive adoption.
verses.kjv: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.

verses.greek: ὅτι δέ ἐστε υἱοί, ἐξαπέστειλεν ὁ θεὸς τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰς καρδίας ⸀ἡμῶν, κρᾶζον· Αββα ὁ πατήρ.
verses.mistral: You are sons, God has sent forth the Spirit of His Son into your hearts, crying, "Abba, Father."
verses.kjv: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.

verses.greek: ὥστε οὐκέτι εἶ δοῦλος ἀλλὰ υἱός· εἰ δὲ υἱός, καὶ κληρονόμος ⸂διὰ θεοῦ⸃.
verses.mistral: You are no longer a slave, but a son; and if a son, then an heir through God.
verses.kjv: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

verses.greek: Ἀλλὰ τότε μὲν οὐκ εἰδότες θεὸν ἐδουλεύσατε τοῖς ⸂φύσει μὴ⸃ οὖσι θεοῖς·
verses.mistral: Then, not knowing God, you were enslaved to those who by nature are not gods.
verses.kjv: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.

verses.greek: νῦν δὲ γνόντες θεόν, μᾶλλον δὲ γνωσθέντες ὑπὸ θεοῦ, πῶς ἐπιστρέφετε πάλιν ἐπὶ τὰ ἀσθενῆ καὶ πτωχὰ στοιχεῖα, οἷς πάλιν ἄνωθεν ⸀δουλεύειν θέλετε;
verses.mistral: Now that you know God, or rather are known by God, how can you turn back again to the weak and impoverished elements, whom you want to enslave all over again?
verses.kjv: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?

verses.greek: ἡμέρας παρατηρεῖσθε καὶ μῆνας καὶ καιροὺς καὶ ἐνιαυτούς.
verses.mistral: Days, months, and seasons and years
verses.kjv: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.

verses.greek: φοβοῦμαι ὑμᾶς μή πως εἰκῇ κεκοπίακα εἰς ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Fear not that I have only slightly in vain labored for you.
verses.kjv: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.

verses.greek: Γίνεσθε ὡς ἐγώ, ὅτι κἀγὼ ὡς ὑμεῖς, ἀδελφοί, δέομαι ὑμῶν. οὐδέν με ἠδικήσατε·
verses.mistral: Become as I am, for I also have become as you, brethren, beseech you. You have wronged me none.
verses.kjv: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.

verses.greek: οἴδατε δὲ ὅτι δι’ ἀσθένειαν τῆς σαρκὸς εὐηγγελισάμην ὑμῖν τὸ πρότερον,
verses.mistral: You know that through weakness of the flesh I preached to you previously
verses.kjv: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.

verses.greek: καὶ τὸν πειρασμὸν ⸀ὑμῶν ἐν τῇ σαρκί μου οὐκ ἐξουθενήσατε οὐδὲ ἐξεπτύσατε, ἀλλὰ ὡς ἄγγελον θεοῦ ἐδέξασθέ με, ὡς Χριστὸν Ἰησοῦν.
verses.mistral: You have not despised or rejected my trial in the flesh, but have received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: ⸀ποῦ ⸀οὖν ὁ μακαρισμὸς ὑμῶν; μαρτυρῶ γὰρ ὑμῖν ὅτι εἰ δυνατὸν τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ὑμῶν ⸀ἐξορύξαντες ἐδώκατέ μοι.
verses.mistral: Where then is the blessing you are hoping for? I testify to you that if possible, even eyes you could have plucked out, I would have given them to you.
verses.kjv: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.

verses.greek: ὥστε ἐχθρὸς ὑμῶν γέγονα ἀληθεύων ὑμῖν;
verses.mistral: I have become your enemy, I am telling you truthfully.
verses.kjv: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?

verses.greek: ζηλοῦσιν ὑμᾶς οὐ καλῶς, ἀλλὰ ἐκκλεῖσαι ὑμᾶς θέλουσιν, ἵνα αὐτοὺς ζηλοῦτε.
verses.mistral: Desire you not to imitate them falsely, but wish to shut you out, that they may be zealous for you.
verses.kjv: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.

verses.greek: καλὸν ⸀δὲ ζηλοῦσθαι ἐν καλῷ πάντοτε, καὶ μὴ μόνον ἐν τῷ παρεῖναί με πρὸς ὑμᾶς,
verses.mistral: It is good always to be zealous in a good cause, and not only to be present with you.
verses.kjv: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.

verses.greek: ⸀τέκνα μου, οὓς πάλιν ὠδίνω ⸀μέχρις οὗ μορφωθῇ Χριστὸς ἐν ὑμῖν·
verses.mistral: My children, for whom I am again in the pains of childbirth until Christ is formed in you
verses.kjv: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,

verses.greek: ἤθελον δὲ παρεῖναι πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἄρτι, καὶ ἀλλάξαι τὴν φωνήν μου, ὅτι ἀποροῦμαι ἐν ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: I desired to be with you now, and to change my tone, because I am in anguish for you.
verses.kjv: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.

verses.greek: Λέγετέ μοι, οἱ ὑπὸ νόμον θέλοντες εἶναι, τὸν νόμον οὐκ ἀκούετε;
verses.mistral: Do you, who desire to be under the law, not hear the law?
verses.kjv: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?

verses.greek: γέγραπται γὰρ ὅτι Ἀβραὰμ δύο υἱοὺς ἔσχεν, ἕνα ἐκ τῆς παιδίσκης καὶ ἕνα ἐκ τῆς ἐλευθέρας·
verses.mistral: Written that Abraham had two sons, one by a slave-girl and one by a free woman.
verses.kjv: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ὁ μὲν ἐκ τῆς παιδίσκης κατὰ σάρκα γεγέννηται, ὁ δὲ ἐκ τῆς ἐλευθέρας ⸀δι’ ἐπαγγελίας.
verses.mistral: Born through a slave-girl naturally, born through a free woman by promise
verses.kjv: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.

verses.greek: ἅτινά ἐστιν ἀλληγορούμενα· αὗται γάρ εἰσιν δύο διαθῆκαι, μία μὲν ἀπὸ ὄρους Σινᾶ, εἰς δουλείαν γεννῶσα, ἥτις ἐστὶν Ἁγάρ,
verses.mistral: These are allegories: They are two covenants; now the one from Mount Sinai was given in bondage, which is Hagar.
verses.kjv: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.

verses.greek: τὸ ⸀δὲ Ἁγὰρ Σινᾶ ὄρος ἐστὶν ἐν τῇ Ἀραβίᾳ, συστοιχεῖ δὲ τῇ νῦν Ἰερουσαλήμ, δουλεύει ⸀γὰρ μετὰ τῶν τέκνων αὐτῆς·
verses.mistral: The Mount Sinai is in Arabia; however, Jerusalem now corresponds to it, for she serves with her children.
verses.kjv: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ἄνω Ἰερουσαλὴμ ἐλευθέρα ἐστίν, ἥτις ἐστὶν ⸀μήτηρ ἡμῶν·
verses.mistral: The higher Jerusalem is free, which is our mother.
verses.kjv: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

verses.greek: γέγραπται γάρ· Εὐφράνθητι, στεῖρα ἡ οὐ τίκτουσα, ῥῆξον καὶ βόησον, ἡ οὐκ ὠδίνουσα· ὅτι πολλὰ τὰ τέκνα τῆς ἐρήμου μᾶλλον ἢ τῆς ἐχούσης τὸν ἄνδρα.
verses.mistral: Mirthful, barren she who has not conceived, tear and cry out, she who does not travail: for the children of the wild are more than those having a husband.
verses.kjv: For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.

verses.greek: ⸂ὑμεῖς δέ, ἀδελφοί, κατὰ Ἰσαὰκ ἐπαγγελίας τέκνα ἐστέ⸃·
verses.mistral: You are children of Isaac by the covenant.
verses.kjv: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ὥσπερ τότε ὁ κατὰ σάρκα γεννηθεὶς ἐδίωκε τὸν κατὰ πνεῦμα, οὕτως καὶ νῦν.
verses.mistral: Likewise then as the one born by flesh pursued the one born by spirit, so also now.
verses.kjv: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ τί λέγει ἡ γραφή; Ἔκβαλε τὴν παιδίσκην καὶ τὸν υἱὸν αὐτῆς, οὐ γὰρ μὴ ⸀κληρονομήσει ὁ υἱὸς τῆς παιδίσκης μετὰ τοῦ υἱοῦ τῆς ἐλευθέρας.
verses.mistral: But what does the Scripture say? Cast out the slave-girl and her son, for the son of the slave-girl will not inherit with the free son.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.

verses.greek: ⸀διό, ἀδελφοί, οὐκ ἐσμὲν παιδίσκης τέκνα ἀλλὰ τῆς ἐλευθέρας.
verses.mistral: Siblings, we are not slaves' children but of the free woman.
verses.kjv: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.

verses.greek: τῇ ἐλευθερίᾳ ⸂ἡμᾶς Χριστὸς ἠλευθέρωσεν· στήκετε οὖν⸃ καὶ μὴ πάλιν ζυγῷ δουλείας ἐνέχεσθε.
verses.mistral: Christ has set us free; stand fast, and do not be subject again to the yoke of slavery.
verses.kjv: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.

verses.greek: Ἴδε ἐγὼ Παῦλος λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐὰν περιτέμνησθε Χριστὸς ὑμᾶς οὐδὲν ὠφελήσει.
verses.mistral: If you are circumcised, Christ will profit you nothing.
verses.kjv: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.

verses.greek: μαρτύρομαι δὲ πάλιν παντὶ ἀνθρώπῳ περιτεμνομένῳ ὅτι ὀφειλέτης ἐστὶν ὅλον τὸν νόμον ποιῆσαι.
verses.mistral: I affirm again to every man who is circumcised that he is debtor to keep the whole law.
verses.kjv: For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

verses.greek: κατηργήθητε ⸀ἀπὸ Χριστοῦ οἵτινες ἐν νόμῳ δικαιοῦσθε, τῆς χάριτος ἐξεπέσατε.
verses.mistral: You have fallen from grace.
verses.kjv: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.

verses.greek: ἡμεῖς γὰρ πνεύματι ἐκ πίστεως ἐλπίδα δικαιοσύνης ἀπεκδεχόμεθα.
verses.mistral: We eagerly await through the Spirit from faith the righteousness of hope.
verses.kjv: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.

verses.greek: ἐν γὰρ Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ οὔτε περιτομή τι ἰσχύει οὔτε ἀκροβυστία, ἀλλὰ πίστις δι’ ἀγάπης ἐνεργουμένη.
verses.mistral: Faith working through love has no distinction.
verses.kjv: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.

verses.greek: Ἐτρέχετε καλῶς· τίς ὑμᾶς ἐνέκοψεν ⸀τῇ ἀληθείᾳ μὴ πείθεσθαι;
verses.mistral: You were running well. Who has hindered you from obeying the truth?
verses.kjv: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?

verses.greek: ἡ πεισμονὴ οὐκ ἐκ τοῦ καλοῦντος ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: The persuasion is not from him who is calling you.
verses.kjv: This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.

verses.greek: μικρὰ ζύμη ὅλον τὸ φύραμα ζυμοῖ.
verses.mistral: Small leaven leavens the whole lump.
verses.kjv: A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.

verses.greek: ἐγὼ πέποιθα εἰς ὑμᾶς ἐν κυρίῳ ὅτι οὐδὲν ἄλλο φρονήσετε· ὁ δὲ ταράσσων ὑμᾶς βαστάσει τὸ κρίμα, ὅστις ⸀ἐὰν ᾖ.
verses.mistral: I have faith in you in the Lord that you will think nothing else; but he who is disturbing you shall bear the judgment, whoever he may be.
verses.kjv: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

verses.greek: ἐγὼ δέ, ἀδελφοί, εἰ περιτομὴν ἔτι κηρύσσω, τί ἔτι διώκομαι; ἄρα κατήργηται τὸ σκάνδαλον τοῦ σταυροῦ.
verses.mistral: I indeed, brethren, if I still preach circumcision, why do I still suffer persecution? Indeed the offense of the cross has been removed.
verses.kjv: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.

verses.greek: ὄφελον καὶ ἀποκόψονται οἱ ἀναστατοῦντες ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Benefit and those being raised up shall be cut off.
verses.kjv: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.

verses.greek: Ὑμεῖς γὰρ ἐπ’ ἐλευθερίᾳ ἐκλήθητε, ἀδελφοί· μόνον μὴ τὴν ἐλευθερίαν εἰς ἀφορμὴν τῇ σαρκί, ἀλλὰ διὰ τῆς ἀγάπης δουλεύετε ἀλλήλοις·
verses.mistral: You were called to freedom, brothers. Only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for self-indulgence, but through love serve one another.
verses.kjv: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ πᾶς νόμος ἐν ἑνὶ λόγῳ ⸀πεπλήρωται, ἐν τῷ· Ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς ⸀σεαυτόν.
verses.mistral: Love your neighbor as yourself.
verses.kjv: For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ἀλλήλους δάκνετε καὶ κατεσθίετε, βλέπετε μὴ ὑπ’ ἀλλήλων ἀναλωθῆτε.
verses.mistral: If indeed you bite and devour one another, take heed lest you be consumed by one another.
verses.kjv: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

verses.greek: Λέγω δέ, πνεύματι περιπατεῖτε καὶ ἐπιθυμίαν σαρκὸς οὐ μὴ τελέσητε.
verses.mistral: Walk by the Spirit, and do not gratify the desires of the flesh.
verses.kjv: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.

verses.greek: ἡ γὰρ σὰρξ ἐπιθυμεῖ κατὰ τοῦ πνεύματος, τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα κατὰ τῆς σαρκός, ταῦτα ⸀γὰρ ⸂ἀλλήλοις ἀντίκειται⸃, ἵνα μὴ ἃ ⸀ἐὰν θέλητε ταῦτα ποιῆτε.
verses.mistral: The flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; for these are contrary to one another, so that you do not do what you wish.
verses.kjv: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ πνεύματι ἄγεσθε, οὐκ ἐστὲ ὑπὸ νόμον.
verses.mistral: If you are guided by the Spirit, you are not under law.
verses.kjv: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.

verses.greek: φανερὰ δέ ἐστιν τὰ ἔργα τῆς σαρκός, ἅτινά ⸀ἐστιν πορνεία, ἀκαθαρσία, ἀσέλγεια,
verses.mistral: Obvious are the works of the flesh, which are fornication, impurity, indecency.
verses.kjv: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,

verses.greek: εἰδωλολατρία, φαρμακεία, ἔχθραι, ⸂ἔρις, ζῆλος⸃, θυμοί, ἐριθεῖαι, διχοστασίαι, αἱρέσεις,
verses.mistral: Idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousy, anger, disputes, divisions, heresies
verses.kjv: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,

verses.greek: ⸀φθόνοι, μέθαι, κῶμοι, καὶ τὰ ὅμοια τούτοις, ἃ προλέγω ὑμῖν ⸀καθὼς προεῖπον ὅτι οἱ τὰ τοιαῦτα πράσσοντες βασιλείαν θεοῦ οὐ κληρονομήσουσιν.
verses.mistral: Envy, drunkenness, revelry, and similar things I am telling you as I have foretold, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ καρπὸς τοῦ πνεύματός ἐστιν ἀγάπη, χαρά, εἰρήνη, μακροθυμία, χρηστότης, ἀγαθωσύνη, πίστις,
verses.mistral: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
verses.kjv: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,

verses.greek: πραΰτης, ἐγκράτεια· κατὰ τῶν τοιούτων οὐκ ἔστιν νόμος.
verses.mistral: Self-control, self-discipline. According to such things, there is no law.
verses.kjv: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ τοῦ ⸀Χριστοῦ τὴν σάρκα ἐσταύρωσαν σὺν τοῖς παθήμασιν καὶ ταῖς ἐπιθυμίαις.
verses.mistral: Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
verses.kjv: And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.

verses.greek: εἰ ζῶμεν πνεύματι, πνεύματι καὶ στοιχῶμεν.
verses.mistral: Live by the Spirit, and do not gratify the desires of the flesh.
verses.kjv: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.

verses.greek: μὴ γινώμεθα κενόδοξοι, ἀλλήλους προκαλούμενοι, ἀλλήλοις φθονοῦντες.
verses.mistral: Do not become vain, but exhort one another, and stir up each other to love and good works.
verses.kjv: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.

verses.greek: Ἀδελφοί, ἐὰν καὶ προλημφθῇ ἄνθρωπος ἔν τινι παραπτώματι, ὑμεῖς οἱ πνευματικοὶ καταρτίζετε τὸν τοιοῦτον ἐν πνεύματι πραΰτητος, σκοπῶν σεαυτόν, μὴ καὶ σὺ πειρασθῇς.
verses.mistral: Fellow believers, if someone is caught in a sin, you who are spiritual should restore him gently, looking to yourself, so that you also may not be tempted.
verses.kjv: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.

verses.greek: ἀλλήλων τὰ βάρη βαστάζετε, καὶ οὕτως ⸀ἀναπληρώσετε τὸν νόμον τοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Carry one another's burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
verses.kjv: Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ δοκεῖ τις εἶναί τι μηδὲν ὤν, ⸂φρεναπατᾷ ἑαυτόν⸃·
verses.mistral: If anyone thinks he can be something without being subject to the law, that man is deceiving himself.
verses.kjv: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ ἔργον ἑαυτοῦ δοκιμαζέτω ἕκαστος, καὶ τότε εἰς ἑαυτὸν μόνον τὸ καύχημα ἕξει καὶ οὐκ εἰς τὸν ἕτερον,
verses.mistral: Each should examine his own work, and then he will have reason for boasting, not against another.
verses.kjv: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

verses.greek: ἕκαστος γὰρ τὸ ἴδιον φορτίον βαστάσει.
verses.mistral: Each shall bear his own load.
verses.kjv: For every man shall bear his own burden.

verses.greek: Κοινωνείτω δὲ ὁ κατηχούμενος τὸν λόγον τῷ κατηχοῦντι ἐν πᾶσιν ἀγαθοῖς.
verses.mistral: The one being instructed should share in all good things with the one doing the instructing.
verses.kjv: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.

verses.greek: μὴ πλανᾶσθε, θεὸς οὐ μυκτηρίζεται· ὃ γὰρ ⸀ἐὰν σπείρῃ ἄνθρωπος, τοῦτο καὶ θερίσει·
verses.mistral: Do not be deceived, God does not be mocked; for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.
verses.kjv: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.

verses.greek: ὅτι ὁ σπείρων εἰς τὴν σάρκα ἑαυτοῦ ἐκ τῆς σαρκὸς θερίσει φθοράν, ὁ δὲ σπείρων εἰς τὸ πνεῦμα ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος θερίσει ζωὴν αἰώνιον.
verses.mistral: Sowing to the flesh results in corruption, but sowing to the Spirit produces eternal life.
verses.kjv: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ καλὸν ποιοῦντες μὴ ⸀ἐγκακῶμεν, καιρῷ γὰρ ἰδίῳ θερίσομεν μὴ ἐκλυόμενοι.
verses.mistral: Reaping what is good, we do not become discouraged, for at the right time we will reap, if we do not give up.
verses.kjv: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.

verses.greek: ἄρα οὖν ὡς καιρὸν ⸀ἔχομεν, ἐργαζώμεθα τὸ ἀγαθὸν πρὸς πάντας, μάλιστα δὲ πρὸς τοὺς οἰκείους τῆς πίστεως.
verses.mistral: Therefore, as opportunity comes, we work for the good of all, but especially for those related to the faith.
verses.kjv: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.

verses.greek: Ἴδετε πηλίκοις ὑμῖν γράμμασιν ἔγραψα τῇ ἐμῇ χειρί.
verses.mistral: You have been written about in large letters with my own hand.
verses.kjv: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.

verses.greek: ὅσοι θέλουσιν εὐπροσωπῆσαι ἐν σαρκί, οὗτοι ἀναγκάζουσιν ὑμᾶς περιτέμνεσθαι, μόνον ⸀ἵνα τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ ⸀Χριστοῦ ⸀μὴ διώκωνται·
verses.mistral: Those desiring to appear in the flesh compel you to be circumcised, only that in the cross of Christ they may not be persecuted.
verses.kjv: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.

verses.greek: οὐδὲ γὰρ οἱ ⸀περιτεμνόμενοι αὐτοὶ νόμον φυλάσσουσιν, ἀλλὰ θέλουσιν ὑμᾶς περιτέμνεσθαι ἵνα ἐν τῇ ὑμετέρᾳ σαρκὶ καυχήσωνται.
verses.mistral: Uncircumcised men, in fact, do not keep the law; rather, they want you to be circumcised so that they may boast against you in your flesh.
verses.kjv: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

verses.greek: ἐμοὶ δὲ μὴ γένοιτο καυχᾶσθαι εἰ μὴ ἐν τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ, δι’ οὗ ἐμοὶ κόσμος ἐσταύρωται ⸀κἀγὼ κόσμῳ.
verses.mistral: It is not I who am boasting, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
verses.kjv: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

verses.greek: ⸂οὔτε γὰρ⸃ περιτομή τί ⸀ἐστιν οὔτε ἀκροβυστία, ἀλλὰ καινὴ κτίσις.
verses.mistral: New creation.
verses.kjv: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.

verses.greek: καὶ ὅσοι τῷ κανόνι τούτῳ στοιχήσουσιν, εἰρήνη ἐπ’ αὐτοὺς καὶ ἔλεος, καὶ ἐπὶ τὸν Ἰσραὴλ τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: And to those who are under the law, peace and mercy, and to the Israelites of God.
verses.kjv: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

verses.greek: Τοῦ λοιποῦ κόπους μοι μηδεὶς παρεχέτω, ἐγὼ γὰρ τὰ στίγματα ⸀τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ σώματί μου βαστάζω.
verses.mistral: I bear in my body the marks of Jesus.
verses.kjv: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

verses.greek: Ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματος ὑμῶν, ἀδελφοί· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: Grace be to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ, brethren. Amen.
verses.kjv: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
